title: acetosella ovata
intro: Acetosella ovata, also known as bog bilberry, is a low-growing shrub that belongs to the heath family. It is native to the wetlands of the British Isles and is characterized by its small, blue-black berries and needle-like leaves. The medicinal properties of Acetosella ovata include its ability to act as an astringent and anti-inflammatory agent, which makes it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant's herbal preparations can be consumed as a tea or used topically in the form of creams and ointments, offering health benefits such as improved digestion and reduced inflammation, as well as protection against certain types of infections.
